subject: Human Resources
title: Parental Leave
description: A micro-lesson that makes Parental Leave usable.

hook.text: The U.S. is the only wealthy nation with no federal paid parental leave. New parents choose between income and bonding.

buildup.text: Parental leave allows new parents—mothers and fathers—to take paid or unpaid time off after birth or adoption.

discovery.text: Companies offering generous parental leave see higher retention, especially among women returning to work.

twist.text: Even when paternity leave exists, men often don't take it—stigma and career fears keep fathers at their desks.

climax.text: Normalizing leave for all parents—and leaders taking it visibly—changes culture faster than any policy change alone.

punchline.text: Parental leave supports families. Using it requires cultural permission.

quiz.question: Why do many fathers not take available paternity leave?
options: Stigma and fear of career consequences
